---
title: "VedicDateTime"
subtitle: "Vedic calendar (Panchanga) computations for R"
---

::: {.hero}

![](assets/VedicDateTimeLOGO2.png){width=160}

<div>

# VedicDateTime

<p class="lead">
An open-source R package to compute key entities from the <b>Vedic (lunisolar) calendar</b> and convert <b>Gregorian</b> dates (or <b>Julian Day Numbers</b>) into <b>Panchanga</b> components such as <b>tithi</b>, <b>vaara</b>, <b>nakshatra</b>, <b>yoga</b>, and <b>karana</b>.
</p>

**CRAN:** [cran.r-project.org/package=VedicDateTime](https://cran.r-project.org/package=VedicDateTime)  
**GitHub:** [github.com/neerajdhanraj/VedicDateTime](https://github.com/neerajdhanraj/VedicDateTime)

[Getting started](getting-started.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
[Cookbook](cookbook.qmd){.btn .btn-secondary}
[API (pkgdown)](pkgdown/){.btn .btn-secondary}

</div>

:::

![](assets/clipboard-2026-05-11_10-09-35.png){fig-alt="Screenshot"}

## Package status

[![CRAN status](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/VedicDateTime)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=VedicDateTime)

## What you get

<div class="cards">

<div class="card">

### Panchanga entities

Compute:

- tithi
- vaara
- nakshatra
- yoga
- karana

</div>

<div class="card">

### Conversions

- Gregorian → JD (`gregorian_to_jd()`)
- JD → Gregorian (`jd_to_gregorian()`)

</div>

<div class="card">

### Location-aware results

Many computations depend on:

- latitude
- longitude
- timezone offset

</div>

<div class="card">

### Time-series features

Transform timestamps into Vedic features (tithi/nakshatra/yoga/masa) for modeling pipelines.

</div>

<div class="card">

### Documentation

- [Reference overview](reference.qmd)
- [API (pkgdown)](pkgdown/)
- [Vignettes](articles.qmd)

</div>

</div>

## Quick start

```r
install.packages("VedicDateTime")

library(VedicDateTime)
place <- c(15.34, 75.13, +5.5)
jd <- gregorian_to_jd(8, 9, 2022)

tithi(jd, place)
```

## Use cases

- Calendar-aware feature engineering for time series
- Research workflows requiring lunisolar calendar entities
- Reproducible calendrical computations for a specified location

## Citation

If you use VedicDateTime in academic work, please cite:

> Bokde, N. D., Patil, P. K., Sengupta, S., Sawant, M., & Feijóo-Lorenzo, A. E. (2023). *VedicDateTime: An R package to implement Vedic calendar system*. Multimedia Tools and Applications. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11042-023-16553-w

## Key links

- Getting started: [getting-started.qmd](getting-started.qmd)
- Cookbook: [cookbook.qmd](cookbook.qmd)
- Accuracy & conventions: [accuracy.qmd](accuracy.qmd)
- API docs: [pkgdown/](pkgdown/)
- Publication + PDF: [publications.qmd](publications.qmd)
